summary: total doses administered to each group must not exceed group population
weekly site capacity not exceeded (sum of 1st and 2nd doses)
weekly vaccine supply not exceeded per vaccine type
VP (ultracold) can only be administered at sites with ultralow freezer
VM (standard freezer) can only be administered at sites with standard or ultralow freezer
VP 2nd dose exactly 3 weeks after 1st dose at same site and group
VM 2nd dose exactly 4 weeks after 1st dose at same site and group
VJ has zero second doses (single-dose vaccine)
2nd doses must use the same vaccine type as 1st dose (no mixing)
1st doses must leave enough horizon for 2nd dose (VP w<=7
G1 must reach >=80% coverage before G2 starts (simplified: G2 may start at w>=3)
G2 must reach >=50% coverage before G4 starts (simplified: G4 may start at w>=4)
each group cumulative coverage is monotonic (implied by non-negative doses)
no group exceeds 100% coverage of population
low-staffing sites (S5 S6) can only administer single-dose VJ
all 1st doses of 2-dose vaccines must have matching 2nd doses scheduled
per site-week dose counts are non-negative integers
cumulative doses used of vaccine v by week w <= cumulative supply by week w
maximize total people vaccinated (coverage)
prioritize high-rank groups (weighted by 1/priority_rank)
minimize wasted doses (use as much supply as possible)
balance vaccinations across sites (fairness)
earliest completion for each group (reward early doses for high-priority groups)
minimize ultralow storage usage (VP is expensive to transport)
